channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Use Gmail on your iPhone? A handy new Gemini-powered search tool has just gone live
item.description: Users on iOS are no longer left out when it comes to using AI to search through and query Gmail messages.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tecnología aplicada a la mejora del reciclaje: el caso de Ecoembes
item.description: La digitalización va ganando terreno entre las distintas industrias en España. Cada vez son más las organizaciones que incorporan herramientas basadas en IoT, inteligencia artificial o blockchain, con el objetivo principal de optimizar su desempeño.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Squarespace vs GoDaddy: Which is the better website builder?
item.description: Squarespace and GoDaddy both offer solid website-building capabilities, with Squarespace excelling in design flexibility and GoDaddy standing out for ease of use and speed. Your choice depends on whether you prioritize creative control or quick setup.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple Intelligence Release Date Leaked: What to Expect from AI Features on October 28
item.description: Apple Intelligence release date, AI features, and what to expect from iOS 18.1 and iPhone 16 lineup
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: 5 ways data scientists can prepare now for genAI transformation
item.description: Until recently, data scientists and analysts’ primary deliverables were data visualizations, machine learning models, dashboards, reports, and analytical insights used for storytelling.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule say they have suspended the process of taking applications for its use as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Automation takes center stage in port negotiations
item.description: Dockworkers and longshoremen with the International Longshoremen's Association (ILA) are underscoring a fear being felt across various industries: Will robots replace them at their jobs? The ILA's three-day strike and ongoing labor negotiations have brought the fight against automation technology
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Watch Web Pages for Updates the Easy Way with Visualping
item.description: Discover how Visualpin, a web-based tool, makes it easy to monitor any webpage for updates and receive email alerts with AI-generated summaries.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: Pelosi, tech executives to hold fundraiser for Harris campaign
item.description: Former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) will join tech executives Saturday to hold a fundraiser for Vice President Harris, a source familiar with the matter confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Node.js previews network inspection support
item.description: Node.js v20.18.0, a just-introduced update to the Long-Term Support (LTS) version of the popular asynchronous, event-driven JavaScript runtime, features experimental network inspection support. Introduced October 3, Node.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Alphabet’s Waymo will expand its robotaxi fleet with Hyundai EVs
item.description: Alphabet’s Waymo will add electric vehicles from South Korea’s Hyundai Motor to its robotaxi fleet, the companies said on Friday, as Waymo expands in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google’s ad-tech antitrust trial: Wrapping up the case for the defense
item.description: And now we wait. Last week, Google did its best to refute the US Department of Justice’s case that the company has built a series of monopolies in the markets for ad serving, ad exchanges, and ad networks, and the DOJ made its rebuttal. Closing arguments will be heard Nov.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple, Amkor, and TSMC ‘neath the Arizona Skies
item.description: It is strategically essential for the US to bring home the manufacture of key components for the technology used across government, consumer, and enterprise markets, an imperative impacting IT leadership that will generate change across the coming decade.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tener prisa por alcanzar el ROI de la IA puede salir caro
item.description: Muchas organizaciones han luchado por encontrar el retorno de la inversión (ROI) después de lanzar proyectos de inteligencia artificial (IA), pero existe el peligro de exigir demasiado, demasiado pronto, según la firma de investigación y asesoramiento de TI Forrester.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Starlink internet service being sent to places hit by Helene
item.description: FEMA said 60 Starlink units have been sent to multiple places devastated by the hurricane, with more to come.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: What’s new for Apple Intelligence?
item.description: Most Apple watchers may have noticed that the company’s iPhone 16 marketing really does put Apple Intelligence front and center, even though its home-baked breed of AaI (Artificial [Apple] Intelligence) isn’t available quite yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The US and Microsoft disrupt a Russian hacking group targeting American officials and nonprofits
item.description: U.S. authorities and Microsoft say hackers linked to Russian intelligence targeted dozens of former military and intelligence officials, journalists and civil society groups with a sophisticated campaign designed to steal information.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google CEO eyeing electricity from nuclear plants for its data centers
item.description: Google CEO Sundar Pichai is considering using electricity from nuclear plants to power his company’s data centers, as the tech giant contends with the expansive energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: The devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene has brought climate change to the forefront of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden signs bill cutting environmental reviews for semiconductor industry
item.description: President Biden on Wednesday signed legislation exempting semiconductor manufacturing facilities from environmental reviews — despite objections from some Democrats and green activists who have expressed concerns about the potential for toxic waste.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: After Change Healthcare, HHS building cyber incident response team
item.description: In the wake of the Change Healthcare ransomware attack, HHS has been expanding its "one-stop shop" for working with the healthcare sector on cyber issues. The post After Change Healthcare, HHS building cyber incident response team first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 23 having arrived September 17, work already has begun on JDK 24, with three features so far proposed for the release: warnings to prepare developers for future restrictions on the use of JNI (Java Native Interface) and a late barrier expansion for the G1 garbage collector
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Surgeons use PlayStation controller for long-distance endoscopy
item.description: Surgeons performed endoscopy on a pig 5,780 miles away using a video game controller. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson has the details on this groundbreaking experiment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Making use of command history on Linux
item.description: Command history on Linux allows you to review and repeat previous commands with little effort. It is built into the bash shell (included in the shell executable).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile service outages reported by users
item.description: People in multiple cities posted about issues they were having, with many saying their phones were in "SOS" mode.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation
